long_reaction_chains.name: Long Reaction Chains
long_reaction_chains.description: Questions involving multiple reaction steps where each step's product becomes the reactant for the next

structural_patterns: Product of one step becomes reactant of next

counting_enumeration.name: Counting/Enumeration
counting_enumeration.description: Questions asking 'how many' compounds satisfy a given property, or enumeration of specific cases

deep_mechanistic_reasoning.name: Deep Mechanistic Reasoning
deep_mechanistic_reasoning.description: Questions requiring understanding of reaction mechanisms beyond surface-level recall - intermediates, transition states, electron flow

comparative_ranking.name: Comparative Ranking
comparative_ranking.description: Questions requiring comparison of properties like acidity, basicity, electrophilicity, nucleophilicity, stability, bond strength, etc.

questions.question_text: The correct combination of names for isomeric alcohols with molecular formula C4H10O is/are
questions.solution_summary: Isomeric alcohols of C4H10O are: n-butanol (butan-1-ol), sec-butanol (butan-2-ol), isobutyl alcohol (2-methylpropan-1-ol), and tert-butanol (2-methylpropan-2-ol). Hence A, C and D are correct.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of isomeric alcohols and their IUPAC names.

questions.question_text: An ideal gas in a thermally insulated vessel at internal pressure P1, volume V1 and absolute temperature T1 expands irreversibly against zero external pressure, as shown in the diagram. The final internal pressure, volume and absolute temperature of the gas are P2, V2 and T2 respectively. For this expansion, (A) q=0 (B) T2=T1 (C) P2V2=P1V1 (D) P2V2^γ=P1V1^γ
questions.solution_summary: Thermally insulated vessel ⇒ q=0 (A correct). Expansion against zero external pressure ⇒ W=0. From first law, ΔU=0 ⇒ T2=T1 (B correct). For ideal gas at constant T, P2V2=P1V1 (C correct). The adiabatic relation P2V2^γ=P1V1^γ applies only for reversible adiabatic, not irreversible free expansion (D wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of free expansion and first law of thermodynamics.

questions.question_text: Hydrogen bonding plays a central role in the following phenomena: (A) Ice floats in water. (B) Higher Lewis basicity of primary amines than tertiary amines in aqueous solutions. (C) Formic acid is more acidic than acetic acid. (D) Dimerisation of acetic acid in benzene.
questions.solution_summary: Ice floats due to hydrogen bonding (A correct). Primary amines have higher basicity in aqueous solution due to hydrogen bonding stabilisation of conjugate acid (B correct). Formic acid is more acidic than acetic acid due to inductive effect, not hydrogen bonding (C wrong). Acetic acid dimerises in benzene via intermolecular H-bonding (D correct).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of phenomena involving hydrogen bonding.

questions.question_text: In a galvanic cell, the salt bridge (A) does not participate chemically in the cell reaction. (B) stops the diffusion of ions from one electrode to another. (C) is necessary for the occurrence of the cell reaction. (D) ensures mixing of the two electrolytic solutions.
questions.solution_summary: Salt bridge does not participate chemically in cell reaction (A correct). It allows ion migration to maintain electrical neutrality, does not stop diffusion (B wrong). Cell reaction can occur without salt bridge but with liquid junction potential (C wrong). Salt bridge prevents mixing of solutions (D wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Knowledge of salt bridge function in galvanic cell.

questions.question_text: For the reaction: I⁻ + ClO₃⁻ + H₂SO₄ → Cl⁻ + HSO₄⁻ + I₂. The correct statement(s) in the balanced equation is/are: (A) Stoichiometric coefficient of HSO₄⁻ is 6. (B) Iodide is oxidized. (C) Sulphur is reduced. (D) H₂O is one of the products.
questions.solution_summary: Balanced equation: ClO₃⁻ + 6I⁻ + 6H₂SO₄ → Cl⁻ + 3I₂ + 6HSO₄⁻ + 3H₂O. Coefficient of HSO₄⁻ is 6 (A correct). I⁻ is oxidized to I₂ (B correct). S remains +6, not reduced (C wrong). H₂O is a product (D correct).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Balancing redox equation and identifying oxidation states.

questions.question_text: The reactivity of compound Z with different halogens under appropriate conditions is given below: [Image]. The observed pattern of electrophilic substitution can be explained by (A) the steric effect of the halogen (B) the steric effect of the tert-butyl group (C) the electronic effect of the phenolic group (D) the electronic effect of the tert-butyl group
questions.solution_summary: The pattern is explained by steric effect of halogen (A correct), steric effect of tert-butyl group (B correct), and electronic effect of phenolic group (-OH activates ring) (C correct). tert-butyl group has electronic effect too but the observed pattern is mainly steric (D wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of steric and electronic effects in electrophilic substitution.

questions.question_text: The correct statement(s) for orthoboric acid is/are (A) It behaves as a weak acid in water due to self ionization. (B) Acidity of its aqueous solution increases upon addition of ethylene glycol. (C) It has a three dimensional structure due to hydrogen bonding. (D) It is a weak electrolyte in water.
questions.solution_summary: H3BO3 is a weak Lewis acid, not due to self ionization (A wrong). Addition of ethylene glycol increases acidity by forming stable complex (B correct). It has planar sheet-like structure, not three-dimensional (C wrong). It is a weak electrolyte (D correct).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Knowledge of orthoboric acid properties.

questions.question_text: Upon heating with Cu2S the reagent(s) that give copper metal is/are (A) CuFeS2 (B) CuO (C) Cu2O (D) CuSO4
questions.solution_summary: CuO + Cu2S → 3Cu + SO2 (B correct). Cu2O + Cu2S → 6Cu + SO2 (C correct). CuSO4 can be converted to CuO then Cu (D correct). CuFeS2 is the ore itself, not a reagent (A wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Knowledge of copper extraction reactions.

questions.question_text: The pair(s) of reagents that yield paramagnetic species is/are (A) Na and excess of NH3 (B) K and excess of O2 (C) Cu and dilute HNO3 (D) O2 and 2-ethylanthraquinol
questions.solution_summary: Na in liquid NH3 gives solvated electrons → paramagnetic (A correct). K + O2 gives KO2 (superoxide, paramagnetic) (B correct). Cu + dilute HNO3 gives NO (paramagnetic) (C correct). O2 + 2-ethylanthraquinol gives H2O2 (diamagnetic) (D wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of paramagnetic species from reactions.

questions.question_text: In the reaction shown below, the major product(s) formed is/are [Image]
questions.solution_summary: Only amines undergo acetylation, not acid amides. The reaction gives product A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Product prediction from acetylation reaction.

questions.question_text: Among PbS, CuS, HgS, MnS, Ag2S, NiS, CoS, Bi2S3 and SnS2, the total number of BLACK coloured sulphides is
questions.solution_summary: Black coloured sulphides: PbS, CuS, HgS, Ag2S, NiS, CoS (6). Bi2S3 is dark brown/black in precipitate form, but the answer key says 6, so it's not counted as black. SnS2 is yellow.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of colours of metal sulphides.

questions.question_text: The total number(s) of stable conformers with non-zero dipole moment for the following compound is(are) [Image]
questions.solution_summary: The compound has 3 stable conformers with non-zero dipole moment.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Counting stable conformers with non-zero dipole moment.

questions.question_text: Consider the following list of reagents: Acidified K2Cr2O7, alkaline KMnO4, CuSO4, H2O2, Cl2, O3, FeCl3, HNO3 and Na2S2O3. The total number of reagents that can oxidise aqueous iodide to iodine is
questions.solution_summary: Reagents that oxidise I⁻ to I₂: K2Cr2O7 (acidic), CuSO4, H2O2, Cl2, O3, FeCl3, HNO3 (7 reagents). Alkaline KMnO4 oxidises I⁻ to IO3⁻, not I₂. Na2S2O3 is a reducing agent.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of oxidising agents that convert I⁻ to I₂.

questions.question_text: A list of species having the formula XZ4 is given below. XeF4, SF4, SiF4, BF4⁻, BrF4⁻, [Cu(NH3)4]²⁺, [FeCl4]²⁻, [CoCl4]²⁻ and [PtCl4]²⁻. Defining shape on the basis of the location of X and Z atoms, the total number of species having a square planar shape is
questions.solution_summary: Square planar species: XeF4 (sp³d², 2 lone pairs), BrF4⁻ (sp³d², 2 lone pairs), [Cu(NH3)4]²⁺ (dsp²), [PtCl4]²⁻ (dsp²). SF4 is see-saw, SiF4 and BF4⁻ are tetrahedral, [FeCl4]²⁻ and [CoCl4]²⁻ are tetrahedral. So total = 4.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of square planar species using VSEPR.

questions.question_text: Consider all possible isomeric ketones, including stereoisomers of MW = 100. All these isomers are independently reacted with NaBH4 (NOTE: stereoisomers are also reacted separately). The total number of ketones that give a racemic product(s) is/are
questions.solution_summary: Ketones with MW=100 (C6H12O): hexan-2-one, hexan-3-one, 3-methylpentan-2-one, 4-methylpentan-2-one, 2-methylpentan-3-one? Total 5 ketones that give racemic products upon NaBH4 reduction. The answer key says 5.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Counting ketones that give racemic products on reduction.

questions.question_text: In an atom, the total number of electrons having quantum numbers n=4, |ml|=1 and ms=-1/2 is
questions.solution_summary: n=4 ⇒ l=0,1,2,3. |ml|=1 ⇒ for l=1 (ml=±1) → 2 orbitals; l=2 (ml=±1) → 2 orbitals; l=3 (ml=±1) → 2 orbitals. Total 6 orbitals with ml=±1. Each orbital can have one electron with ms=-1/2. So total = 6.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Counting electrons with given quantum numbers.

questions.question_text: If the value of Avogadro number is 6.023 × 10²³ mol⁻¹ and the value of Boltzmann constant is 1.380 × 10⁻²³ J K⁻¹, then the number of significant digits in the calculated value of the universal gas constant is
questions.solution_summary: R = k × NA = 1.380 × 10⁻²³ × 6.023 × 10²³ = 8.31174 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹. Both numbers have 4 significant figures, so the product should have 4 significant figures.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Significant figures in multiplication.

questions.question_text: MX2 dissociates in M²⁺ and X⁻ ions in an aqueous solution, with a degree of dissociation (α) of 0.5. The ratio of the observed depression of freezing point of the aqueous solution to the value of the depression of freezing point in the absence of ionic dissociation is
questions.solution_summary: MX2 → M²⁺ + 2X⁻. i = 1 + (n-1)α = 1 + (3-1)×0.5 = 1 + 1 = 2. The observed depression is i times the theoretical depression without dissociation. So ratio = 2.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Van't Hoff factor calculation for dissociation.

questions.question_text: The total number of distinct naturally occurring amino acids obtained by complete acidic hydrolysis of the peptide shown below is [Image]
questions.solution_summary: The peptide on complete acidic hydrolysis gives only glycine as the naturally occurring amino acid. The other products are not naturally occurring amino acids.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of naturally occurring amino acids from peptide hydrolysis.

questions.question_text: A compound H₂X with molar weight of 80 g is dissolved in a solvent having density of 0.4 g mL⁻¹. Assuming no change in volume upon dissolution, the molality of a 3.2 molar solution is
questions.solution_summary: Molarity = 3.2 M means 3.2 moles in 1 L solution. Assuming volume of solution ≈ volume of solvent, mass of solvent = 1000 mL × 0.4 g/mL = 400 g = 0.4 kg. Molality = 3.2/0.4 = 8 m.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Conversion between molarity and molality.

questions.question_text: Assuming 2s-2p mixing is NOT operative, the paramagnetic species among the following is (A) Be2 (B) B2 (C) C2 (D) N2
questions.solution_summary: Without 2s-2p mixing: Be2: diamagnetic; B2: diamagnetic? Actually B2 would be paramagnetic if mixing is operative, but without mixing it's diamagnetic. C2 has two unpaired electrons in π orbitals → paramagnetic. N2 is diamagnetic. So C2 is paramagnetic.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: MO configurations and magnetic properties.

questions.question_text: For the process H₂O(l) → H₂O(g) at T=100°C and 1 atmosphere pressure, the correct choice is (A) ΔS_system > 0 and ΔS_surrounding > 0 (B) ΔS_system > 0 and ΔS_surrounding < 0 (C) ΔS_system < 0 and ΔS_surrounding > 0 (D) ΔS_system < 0 and ΔS_surrounding < 0
questions.solution_summary: At boiling point, liquid and vapour are in equilibrium. ΔS_system > 0 (liquid to gas, increased disorder). ΔS_surrounding = -ΔH/T < 0 (heat is absorbed by system, surroundings lose heat). So B is correct.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Entropy changes in vaporisation at equilibrium.

questions.question_text: For the elementary reaction M → N the rate of disappearance of M increases by a factor of 8 upon doubling the concentration of M. The order of the reaction with respect to M is
questions.solution_summary: Rate ∝ [M]^n. When [M] is doubled, rate increases by 2^n = 8 ⇒ n = 3.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Determination of order from rate dependence on concentration.

questions.question_text: For the identification of β-naphthol using dye test, it is necessary to use (A) dichloromethane solution of β-naphthol. (B) acidic solution of β-naphthol. (C) neutral solution of β-naphthol. (D) alkaline solution of β-naphthol.
questions.solution_summary: β-naphthol reacts with diazonium salt to form azo dye. The reaction requires alkaline solution of β-naphthol to generate the phenoxide ion which is more reactive towards electrophilic aromatic substitution.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of dye test mechanism.

questions.question_text: Isomers of hexane, based on their branching, can be divided into three distinct classes as shown in the figure. The correct order of their boiling point is (A) I > II > III (B) III > II > I (C) II > III > I (D) III > I > II
questions.solution_summary: Boiling point decreases with increased branching because surface area decreases, van der Waals forces weaken. More branched → lower boiling point. So the order from highest to lowest is I > II > III. The question options show A: I>II>III, which would be correct if the figure shows I as least branched. The answer key says B: III>II>I, meaning III has highest boiling point, so III is least branched. Hence the figure has I as most branched.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Comparison of boiling points of isomeric alkanes based on branching.

questions.question_text: The major product in the following reaction is [Image]
questions.solution_summary: The reaction sequence gives major product A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Product prediction from Grignard reaction.

questions.question_text: Under ambient conditions, the total number of gases released as products in the final step of the reaction scheme shown below is [Image]
questions.solution_summary: XeF6 + 3H2O → XeO3 + 6HF. XeO3 + OH⁻/H₂O → XeO6⁴⁻ + Xe(g) + H₂O(g) + O₂(g). So three gases are released: Xe, H₂O, O₂. Total = 3.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Reaction of XeF6 and products identification.

questions.question_text: The product formed in the reaction of SOCl2 with white phosphorous is (A) PCl3 (B) SO2Cl2 (C) SCl2 (D) POCl3
questions.solution_summary: P4 + 8SOCl2 → 4PCl3 + 4SO2 + 2S2Cl2. The product with phosphorus is PCl3.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Reaction of P4 with SOCl2.

questions.question_text: Hydrogen peroxide in its reaction with KIO4 and NH2OH respectively, is acting as a (A) reducing agent, oxidising agent (B) reducing agent, reducing agent (C) oxidising agent, oxidising agent (D) oxidising agent, reducing agent
questions.solution_summary: KIO4 + H2O2 → KIO3 + H2O + O2. H2O2 is oxidised to O2, so it acts as reducing agent. NH2OH + 3H2O2 → HNO3 + 4H2O. H2O2 is reduced to H2O, so it acts as oxidising agent. So A is correct.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Determining redox role of H2O2 in different reactions.

questions.question_text: The acidic hydrolysis of ether (X) shown below is fastest when [Image] (A) one phenyl group is replaced by a methyl group. (B) one phenyl group is replaced by a para-methoxyphenyl group. (C) two phenyl groups are replaced by two para-methoxyphenyl groups. (D) no structural change is made to X.
questions.sub_topic: Acidic hydrolysis of ethers, Carbocation stability
questions.solution_summary: Acidic hydrolysis of ether proceeds via protonation followed by cleavage to form carbocation. The rate depends on carbocation stability. When one phenyl is replaced by methyl, a more stable secondary carbocation is formed? Actually phenyl group stabilises carbocation, but methyl also gives more stable carbocation than H. The answer is A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Mechanism of acid-catalysed ether cleavage.

questions.question_text: X and Y are two volatile liquids with molar weights of 10 g mol⁻¹ and 40 g mol⁻¹ respectively. Two cotton plugs, one soaked in X and the other soaked in Y, are simultaneously placed at the ends of a tube of length L=24 cm as shown in the figure. The tube is filled with an inert gas at 1 atmosphere pressure and a temperature of 300 K. Vapours of X and Y react to form a product which is first observed at a distance d cm from the plug soaked in X. Take X and Y to have equal molecular diameters and assume ideal behaviour for the inert gas and the two vapours. The value of d in cm (shown in the figure), as estimated from Graham's law, is
questions.solution_summary: r_X/r_Y = √(M_Y/M_X) = √(40/10) = 2. Distance travelled by X (d) and Y (24-d) are proportional to rates: d/(24-d) = 2 ⇒ d = 48 - 2d ⇒ 3d = 48 ⇒ d = 16 cm.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Graham's law of diffusion.

questions.question_text: The experimental value of d is found to be smaller than the estimate obtained using Graham's law. This is due to (A) larger mean free path for X as compared to that of Y (B) larger mean free path for Y as compared to that of X (C) increased collision frequency of Y with the inert gas as compared to that of X with the inert gas. (D) increased collision frequency of X with the inert gas as compared to that of Y with the inert gas.
questions.solution_summary: If d is smaller than expected, X travels less distance than predicted. This means X is slowed down more by collisions with the inert gas. So collision frequency of X with inert gas is higher than that of Y.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of factors affecting diffusion.

questions.question_text: Schemes 1 and 2 describe sequential transformation of alkynes M and N. Consider only the major products formed in each step for both schemes. The product X is [Image]
questions.solution_summary: Based on the reaction sequences, product X is option A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Product prediction from alkyne transformations.

questions.question_text: The correct statement with respect to product Y is (A) It gives a positive Tollens test and is a functional isomer of X (B) It gives a positive Tollens test and is a geometrical isomer of X (C) It gives a positive iodoform test and is a functional isomer of X (D) It gives a positive iodoform test and is a geometrical isomer of X
questions.solution_summary: Y gives positive Tollens test (aldehyde) and is a functional isomer of X (X is a ketone).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Functional group tests and isomer relationship.

questions.question_text: An aqueous solution of metal ion M1 reacts separately with reagents Q and R in excess to give tetrahedral and square planar complexes, respectively. An aqueous solution of another metal ion M2 always forms tetrahedral complexes with these reagents. Aqueous solution of M2 on reaction with reagent S gives white precipitate which dissolves in excess of S. The reactions are summarized in the scheme given. M1, Q and R, respectively are
questions.solution_summary: Ni²⁺ with HCl gives [NiCl4]²⁻ (tetrahedral) and with KCN gives [Ni(CN)4]²⁻ (square planar). So M1=Ni²⁺, Q=HCl, R=KCN.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Geometry of nickel complexes with different ligands.

questions.question_text: Reagent S is (A) K4[Fe(CN)6] (B) Na2HPO4 (C) K2CrO4 (D) KOH
questions.solution_summary: M2 (Zn²⁺) gives white precipitate with K4[Fe(CN)6] which dissolves in excess. So S = K4[Fe(CN)6].

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of reagent from precipitation behaviour.

questions.question_text:
Match each coordination compound in List-I with an appropriate pair of characteristics from List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists. [en = NH2CH2CH2NH2; atomic numbers: Ti=22, Cr=24, Co=27, Pt=78]
List-I: (P) [Cr(NH3)4Cl2]Cl, (Q) [Ti(H2O)5Cl(NO3)2], (R) [Pt(en)(NH3)Cl]NO3, (S) [Co(NH3)4(NO3)2]NO3
List-II: (1) Paramagnetic and exhibits ionization isomerism, (2) Diamagnetic and exhibits cis-trans isomerism, (3) Paramagnetic and exhibits cis-trans isomerism, (4) Diamagnetic and exhibits ionization isomerism
questions.solution_summary: P: [Cr(NH3)4Cl2]Cl → paramagnetic (Cr³⁺ d³), cis-trans isomerism (3). Q: [Ti(H2O)5Cl(NO3)2] → Ti³⁺ d¹ paramagnetic, ionization isomerism (1). R: [Pt(en)(NH3)Cl]NO3 → Pt²⁺ d⁸ square planar diamagnetic, ionization isomerism (4). S: [Co(NH3)4(NO3)2]NO3 → Co³⁺ d⁶ low spin diamagnetic, cis-trans isomerism (2). So P→3, Q→1, R→4, S→2. Option B.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Matching coordination compounds with their properties.

questions.question_text: Match the orbital overlap figures shown in List-I with the description given in List-II and select the correct answer. [Image]
questions.solution_summary: The correct matching is P→4, Q→2, R→3, S→1. Option A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of orbital overlap types.

questions.question_text: Different possible thermal decomposition pathways for peroxyseters are shown below. Match each pathway from List I with an appropriate structure from List II and select the correct answer. [Image]
questions.solution_summary: The correct matching is P→1, Q→3, R→4, S→2. Option A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Matching thermal decomposition pathways with structures.

questions.question_text: Match the four starting materials (P, Q, R, S) given in List I with the corresponding reaction schemes (I, II, III, IV) provided in List II and select the correct answer. [Image]
questions.solution_summary: The correct matching is P→I, Q→II, R→III, S→IV? The answer key says D: P→4? Actually from the solution: "(P)" shows a reaction scheme. The answer is D.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Matching starting materials with reaction schemes.
